bc_alignment.big_idea: Living things have features and behaviours that help them survive in their environment

covered: 1A-DA-06: Collect and present data in visual formats
1A-AP-10: Decompose steps needed to solve problems
partially_covered: 1A-AP-08: Model daily processes by creating algorithms

missing: 1A-CS-01: Select and operate appropriate tools to express ideas using digital representations
1A-AP-12: Develop plans that describe sequence of events, goals, expected outcomes
1A-IC-13: Identify ways people use computing in daily life
1A-IC-14: Identify positive and negative impacts of computing

strengths: BC Science Big Idea fully addressed
Sorting activity aligns with CSTA data analysis standards
Classification task requires decomposition of problem
gaps: No explicit connection to computing as everyday tool (1A-CS-01)
No explicit positive/negative impacts of computing discussion (1A-IC-13, 1A-IC-14)
Algorithm planning step not explicitly included (1A-AP-12)

recommendations: Add reflection question: 'How do computers help us learn about living things?'
Include collaborative sorting activity where students work in pairs
Add planning worksheet where students describe their sorting approach before coding
